skill.name: use-gemini-for-code-analysis
skill.description: Uses Gemini CLI to analyze large codebases, inspect architecture, trace features, and return the raw Gemini output.
skill.summary: Run Gemini CLI across a repository for broad architecture and code analysis.

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified that the skill instructs the agent to run Gemini CLI commands against the full codebase. This is a legitimate code-analysis wrapper, but it creates external command and data exposure risk because repository contents may be sent to Gemini CLI. The weak cryptography and entropy alerts are false positives; no evidence found of cryptographic code, encoded payloads, prompt injection, or malicious intent in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External Gemini CLI Execution Over Full Repository
medium_findings.description: The skill explicitly instructs the agent to use Bash to run Gemini CLI with --all-files. This is intended behavior, but it may expose full repository contents to an external AI tool and depends on the local gemini command configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions and examples directly call gemini --all-files through Bash. The risk is clear, but it is also the stated purpose of this code-analysis wrapper.

medium_findings.title: Optional Confirmation Bypass Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill suggests considering --yolo to skip confirmations for non-destructive tasks. This can reduce user oversight if the Gemini CLI or prompt triggers actions beyond analysis.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly recommends considering --yolo. The instruction limits it to non-destructive tasks, so the risk depends on operator judgment and Gemini CLI behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static alert at the description line appears to be a false positive. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, weak algorithms, or security-sensitive hashing in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited line shows natural-language metadata only. There is no cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection to evaluate.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Alert Dismissed
low_findings.description: The entropy heuristic appears to be caused by non-English front matter and dense descriptive text. No evidence found of binary content, encrypted payloads, or encoded commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited area is readable YAML front matter and Chinese text. The file contains no hidden payload or obfuscated executable content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash-Based External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires the agent to invoke Gemini CLI from Bash and provides multiple gemini --all-files command examples. This pattern should be disclosed because it executes local commands and may transmit repository data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is present in explicit instructions and code blocks. The security impact comes from external tool execution and repository-wide data sharing.

content.user_title: Analyze Large Codebases with Gemini CLI
content.value_statement: Large repositories are hard to understand with local inspection alone. This skill routes focused architecture and feature questions through Gemini CLI and returns its raw analysis.
actual_capabilities: Builds Gemini CLI prompts for full-repository analysis.
Uses --all-files for broad codebase context when appropriate.
Supports architecture mapping, pattern discovery, and feature tracing prompts.
Returns raw Gemini CLI output for the caller to interpret.
Guides prompt wording for Chinese-language analysis unless the user asks otherwise.
limitations: Requires Gemini CLI to be installed and authenticated locally.
May send repository contents to an external AI service through Gemini CLI.
Does not verify, summarize, or correct Gemini output.
Does not include safeguards for excluding secrets or private files.

use_cases.title: Map an unfamiliar repository
use_cases.description: Ask Gemini CLI to identify major components, data flow, directory structure, and design decisions across the project.

use_cases.title: Trace a feature implementation
use_cases.description: Find the files, call paths, integrations, and data transformations behind a specific user-facing feature.

use_cases.title: Scan for recurring code patterns
use_cases.description: Search the whole repository for a named pattern, implementation style, or quality concern and collect examples.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner repository overview
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a first pass over an unfamiliar project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to analyze the whole repository with Gemini CLI. Ask for the main modules, entry points, data flow, and important directories.

prompt_templates.title: Feature tracing request
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you know the feature name but not its implementation path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Gemini CLI to trace the complete implementation of [feature name]. Include involved files, control flow, data models, and integration points.

prompt_templates.title: Pattern audit request
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need examples of a framework, convention, or risky pattern.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Gemini CLI to scan all files for [pattern]. Return examples, affected areas, likely intent, and any maintainability concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced architecture review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before a redesign, migration, or major refactor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Gemini CLI to analyze the system architecture. Focus on boundaries, dependency direction, data ownership, coupling, and migration risks.

output_examples.input: Analyze the repository architecture and identify the main components.
output_examples.output: A raw Gemini CLI response describing modules, directories, entry points, data flow, and notable design decisions.

output_examples.input: Trace the billing workflow implementation across the codebase.
output_examples.output: A raw Gemini CLI response listing relevant files, services, models, call paths, and integration points.

output_examples.input: Find all usages of a custom authorization pattern.
output_examples.output: A raw Gemini CLI response with discovered examples, locations, common usage patterns, and potential issues.

best_practices: Review repository contents for secrets before running full-codebase analysis.
Ask narrow questions so Gemini CLI output stays focused and useful.
Validate important findings against source files before making changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use --all-files on repositories that contain secrets or restricted data.
Do not treat raw Gemini output as verified without checking the code.
Do not use confirmation bypass flags for tasks that may edit files or run side effects.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an agent run Gemini CLI for whole-repository code analysis and returns the raw result.

faq.question: Does it modify code?
faq.answer: The documented purpose is analysis. Users should avoid prompts or flags that allow file changes.

faq.question: Why is it rated medium risk?
faq.answer: It runs an external command and may send full repository contents to Gemini CLI.

faq.question: Does it require setup?
faq.answer: Yes. Gemini CLI must be installed, configured, and available in the local shell.

faq.question: Can it analyze private repositories?
faq.answer: It can, but users should confirm data policies and remove secrets before using full-codebase analysis.

faq.question: Does it summarize Gemini results?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs the agent to return Gemini output without secondary interpretation.
